note: Mathlib/Analysis/PSeries.lean:360-362, docstring '**Divergence of the Harmonic Series**' — Tendsto (fun n => ∑ i ∈ Finset.range n, (1/(i+1):ℝ)) atTop atTop; the divergence theorem for the harmonic series, plus harmonic : ℕ → ℚ (partial sums) in NumberTheory/Harmonic/Defs.lean:22.
verify_note: Overclaimed exact: decl is the DIVERGENCE THEOREM (PSeries.lean:361, docstring 'Divergence of the Harmonic Series') about the series, not the series object itself (Wikidata Q464100 defines it via formula sum 1/n); existing catalog/data/concept_layer.jsonl already tags this same decl as secondary evidence for Q906520 'Divergent series', confirming it is evidence-about not identical-to — should be match_kind related, not exact.

note: Mathlib/Analysis/ODE/Basic.lean:54-55, def IsIntegralCurve (γ : ℝ → E) (v : ℝ → E → E) : Prop := ∀ t, HasDerivAt γ (v t (γ t)) t — the definition of γ being a solution to the (possibly time-dependent, vector-valued) first-order ODE γ' = v(t,γ); Mathlib/Analysis/ODE/ also has Picard–Lindelöf existence/uniqueness (ExistUnique.lean, PicardLindelof.lean).
verify_note: Verified def at Mathlib/Analysis/ODE/Basic.lean:54 (HasDerivAt ∀ t) is literally the definitional core of solving a real-parameter (i.e. ORDINARY, single-variable) first-order ODE γ'=v(t,γ); corroborated by existing concept_layer.jsonl which already uses this exact decl as primary_decl (confidence high, status formalized) for the closely related QID Q11214 'Differential equation' — exact is defensible for the ODE-specific QID Q465274.
